depression.description: I can't shake the feeling that I'm failing Ethan as a friend. His behavior is spiraling, and I feel completely helpless to do anything about it.
Every time I think about not being able to help Ethan, I feel like I'm not good enough. It's like my worth is tied to my ability to fix other people's problems.
I've been withdrawing from my friends lately. I feel like I can't connect with anyone because I'm so consumed by worry about Ethan.
I keep blaming myself for not seeing the signs with Ethan. It feels like if I had been a better friend, I could have prevented this.
I've been drinking more than I should and engaging in casual relationships to distract myself. I know it's not healthy, but I just want to escape these feelings.

Delusion.description: I constantly worry that I'm unlovable and that anyone I get close to will end up hurting me or others, just like Ethan is hurting his girlfriend.

Delusion.topic: Belief that I must fix others
Delusion.description: I feel this overwhelming pressure that it's my responsibility to fix Ethan's problems, and if I can't, it means I've failed him and everyone else I care about.
